summary: Excellent sound‑board capture of a solid 1991 show – crisp mix, strong Shakedown opener and tight Bertha/Deal, though the LA Coliseum vibe was chaotic.
review: The digital sound‑board source is crystal clear with superb instrument separation and no of the infamous “swirling” that plagued other copies. Musically the band delivers a respectable performance: a booming Shakedown Street opener, a tight Bertha, a rocking Deal and several well‑executed jams (Playin > UJB, Picasso Moon, Foolish Heart). The show is marred by an aggressive security presence and a tense crowd atmosphere, which colors many fans’ memories, but the playing itself is generally praised.

ai_rating.rationale: Recording quality is consistently rated excellent; show quality is viewed as good but mixed because of venue‑related issues. Weighted combination yields a near‑4‑star rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix with very good instrument separation; the usual “swirling” of Healy’s board is absent. A few listeners recall original venue sound problems, but the tape fixes those issues.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A long, high‑energy Shakedown opener leads a first set that some found uneven; the second set settles into tighter jams (Bertha, Deal, Playin > UJB) and finishes with strong NFA/Throwing Stones. Overall flow is solid musically but hampered by crowd‑control chaos.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for sparkling guitar tone and inspired lead work (especially on Shakedown, Candyman, Bertha).
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted as sounding great, particularly on the Playing > UJB transition.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince is liked by many reviewers; some miss Brent. Hornsby receives praise for his piano work and improvisational flair.
band_member_comments.Drums: Described as typical Bill Kreutzmann – solid and space‑filled, no major complaints.
